title: Lightweight vehicle hvac structure
abstract: An HVAC system includes a blower housing including a blower inlet and a blower outlet, the blower housing having a plastic skeleton structure. The HVAC system further includes a foam blower liner disposed within and coupled to the blower housing.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. An HVAC system comprising: a blower housing comprising a blower inlet and a blower outlet, the blower housing having a plastic skeleton structure; and a foam blower liner disposed within and coupled to the blower housing. 2. The HVAC system of claim 1, wherein: the blower housing further comprises a first body and an opposing second body disposed on the first body; and the blower housing defines a mesh structure. 3. The HVAC system of claim 2, wherein the first body has a material thickness that is greater than a material thickness of the second body. 4. The HVAC system of claim 2, wherein: the blower liner is a three-dimensional foam and defines a flange; and the flange is sandwiched between the first body and the second body. 5. The HVAC system of claim 4, the blower liner is ultrasonically welded to the blower housing proximate the blower inlet. 6. The HVAC system of claim 1, wherein the blower housing defines a mesh structure comprising a solid wall proximate a scroll cut-off defined between a scroll portion and a manifold portion of the blower housing. 7. The HVAC system of claim 6, wherein the foam liner is ultrasonically welded to the blower housing proximate the scroll cut-off. 8. The HVAC system of claim 1 further comprising a blower having a motor and a fan cage, the blower provided in the blower housing. 9. The HVAC system of claim 8, wherein: the first body defines a fan opening configured to receive at least a portion of the blower therethrough; and 

 the blower inlet is configured to receive an ultrasonic welding tool therethrough for ultrasonically welding the blower liner to the blower housing. 10. The HVAC system of claim 9, wherein the blower liner defines: a blower liner inlet complementary to the blower inlet; and a liner fan opening complementary to the fan opening. 11. The HVAC system of claim 1, further comprising an evaporator disposed downstream from the blower housing. 12. The HVAC system of claim 11, further comprising: an evaporator housing comprising an evaporator inlet and an evaporator outlet, the evaporator housing having a plastic skeleton structure; and a foam evaporator liner disposed within and engaging the evaporator housing; wherein the evaporator is disposed in the evaporator liner. 13. The HVAC system of claim 12, wherein at least a portion of the evaporator liner is compressed between the evaporator and the evaporator housing. 14. The HVAC system of claim 12, further comprising an adapter comprising an adapter inlet extending into the blower outlet and an adapter outlet extending into the evaporator inlet. 15. The HVAC system of claim 14, wherein a portion of the blower liner proximate the blower outlet is disposed between the adapter and the blower housing. 16. The HVAC system of claim 14, wherein a portion of the evaporator liner proximate the evaporator inlet is disposed between the adapter and the evaporator housing. 17. The HVAC system of claim 12, wherein: the evaporator housing comprises a first body and an opposing second body disposed on the first body; and at least one of the first or second bodies defines a mesh structure. 18. An HVAC system comprising: an evaporator housing comprising an evaporator inlet and an evaporator outlet, the evaporator housing having a plastic skeleton structure; a closed-cell foam evaporator liner disposed within and engaging an entire inner surface of the evaporator housing; and an evaporator disposed within the evaporator liner. 19. The HVAC system of claim 18, wherein: the evaporator housing further comprises a first body and an opposing second body disposed on the first body; and a substantially vertical seam is located between the first body and the second body; and the seam is sealed from the evaporator by the evaporator liner. 20. The HVAC system of claim 18, wherein the evaporator liner is disposed between the evaporator housing and substantially an entire outer periphery of the evaporator.
